services.title: Expertise

about.title: About UIUXDESIGNER
about.intro: I design and build modern websites for businesses across various sectors.
about.body1: My focus is on clear design, strong structure and websites that convert visitors into customers.
about.body2: Every website is carefully designed with attention to typography, usability and an appearance that fits the company behind it.

contact.description: Tell us about your project and we'll schedule a free consultation to discuss the possibilities.
contact.free_concept: I'd like to receive a free concept
contact.thank_you_message: Your message has been received. We'll get back to you as soon as possible to schedule a call.
contact.success_toast: Message sent! We'll be in touch soon.

faq.description: Answers to the most common questions about collaboration, costs and the process.

items.question: What does a new website cost?
items.answer: The price of a website depends on the scope and complexity of the project. Smaller websites typically start around €1200–€2500, while larger custom projects can be higher. After a brief introduction, I can provide a clear price indication.

items.question: How long does it take to build a website?
items.answer: Most websites are designed and built within 3 to 6 weeks. This depends on the scope of the project and how quickly content such as text and images are available.

items.question: Do you only work with companies from the Netherlands?
items.answer: No. Although I'm based in the Netherlands, I also work with companies from other countries. Most communication is done online, making remote collaboration easy.

items.question: What system do you build websites in?
items.answer: Most websites are developed as custom WordPress websites. This gives businesses a fast, scalable website that's easy to manage. For specific projects, custom solutions are also possible.

items.question: Can I edit my website myself later?
items.answer: Yes. Websites are set up so you can easily edit texts, images and pages without technical knowledge.

items.question: Can you also improve my existing website?
items.answer: Yes. In addition to building new websites, I also help businesses with redesigning or improving existing websites, for example in terms of structure, design or conversion.

items.question: Do you also help with branding, photography or video?
items.answer: Yes. In addition to web design, I can also help with visual identity, photography and video so your website gets a strong and consistent look.

items.question: How does a collaboration start?
items.answer: The process starts with a brief introduction where we discuss your business, goals and wishes. Then you receive a proposal with planning and price. After approval, the website design begins.

caseCta.strong_platform: Need a strong online platform too?
caseCta.complex_platform: Need to make a complex platform user-friendly?

caseGmt.label: Industrial · UI/UX Design · Web Design
caseGmt.title: GMT Equipment
caseGmt.description: Website redesign for GMT Equipment, manufacturer of innovative saw and grab systems. Focus on structure, conversion and strengthening their technical brand identity.
caseGmt.story_title: GMT Equipment is a manufacturer of innovative saw and grab systems for the demolition and recycling industry.
caseGmt.story_p1: Their existing website no longer matched the quality and level of their products. The website lacked structure, had unclear navigation and was not optimized for conversion.
caseGmt.story_p2: The assignment: a complete website redesign that strengthens GMT's technical character, presents products clearly and guides visitors to a quote request or contact moment.
caseGmt.wireframes_title: From sitemap to wireframe: a foundation for conversion.
caseGmt.wireframes_p1: Based on target audience analysis and business objectives, a new sitemap was set up. Each page is designed with a clear hierarchy and call-to-actions that guide visitors toward conversion.
caseGmt.wireframes_p2: The wireframes were iteratively built and validated, so the structure is solid before visual design begins.
caseGmt.design_title: Visual design that strengthens GMT's technical character.
caseGmt.design_p1: The design combines industrial power with modern minimalism. Dark backgrounds emphasize GMT's yellow brand color and create a premium look that fits their market position.
caseGmt.design_p2: Typography, color use and white space are deliberately used to present technical products clearly and convincingly.
caseGmt.process_title: Design, test and improve in short cycles.
caseGmt.process_p1: The design process went through multiple rounds. Each iteration was discussed with the GMT team to process feedback and bring the design closer to the ideal user experience.
caseGmt.process_p2: By sharing and validating prototypes early, major adjustments during the build phase were prevented.
caseGmt.ux_title: User experience as the basis for every design decision.
caseGmt.ux_p1: Navigation was simplified and the product overview completely restructured. Visitors now find the right product in two clicks, including technical specifications and a direct quote option.
caseGmt.ux_p2: Mobile user experience received extra attention, as a large part of the target audience visits the website on-site via phone.
caseGmt.result_title: A website that radiates GMT's power.
caseGmt.result_p1: The end result is a fully responsive website that performs strongly on every device. Product pages are optimized for both information gathering and conversion.
caseGmt.result_p2: The new website strengthens GMT's professional image and provides a solid foundation for future growth and expansion of their online presence.
caseGmt.decisions_p1: Every visual choice is substantiated: from the dark color palettes that communicate industrial power, to the typography that radiates technical reliability.
caseGmt.decisions_p2: GMT's yellow accent color is strategically used as a visual guide, making call-to-actions and important elements stand out immediately without disturbing the design's calm.

caseMaxled.label: E-commerce · UI/UX Design · Webshop
caseMaxled.title: MaxLED
caseMaxled.description: E-commerce platform redesign for the #1 in rental and sales of fixed and mobile LED screens. Focus on product presentation, usability and quote requests.
caseMaxled.ux_title: Analyzing the old webshop and exposing pain points.
caseMaxled.ux_p1: We analyzed the old webshop on mobile and desktop, reviewed Google Analytics and conducted competitive research. The biggest pain points were unclear product pages, slow load times and visually cluttered interfaces.
caseMaxled.ux_p2: The goal: develop a new webshop for MaxLED that clearly presents the wide range of LED lighting, builds trust and is focused on conversion.
caseMaxled.before_title: From outdated to modern: a completely new platform.
caseMaxled.before_p1: The old website lacked a clear structure and professional appearance. The new website combines the brand's strength with an intuitive user experience.
caseMaxled.before_p2: The result is a platform that radiates trust and effortlessly guides visitors to the right product.
caseMaxled.visual_title: A visual system that strengthens the brand on every screen.
caseMaxled.visual_p1: The visual design combines the distinctive orange brand color with dark backgrounds for a premium look. Every element is designed to put the products center stage.
caseMaxled.visual_p2: From the homepage to product pages and rental formulas — everything is visually consistent and focused on conversion.
caseMaxled.uxapproach_title: The UX approach behind the design.
caseMaxled.uxapproach_p1: We analyzed the old webshop on mobile and desktop, reviewed Google Analytics and conducted competitive research. The biggest pain points were unclear navigation, slow load times and visually cluttered interfaces.
caseMaxled.uxapproach_p2: We restructured the navigation and product hierarchy into clear categories (e.g., LED strips, spots, accessories). Filters were rebuilt based on product characteristics such as screen area, status and type.
caseMaxled.ecommerce_title: Clickable prototypes for the most important user flows.
caseMaxled.ecommerce_p1: We created clickable Figma prototypes of the most essential user flows, including searching for products, adding to cart, and checkout. This allowed us to process internal feedback quickly.
caseMaxled.ecommerce_p2: We designed a modern, light interface with a technical and trustworthy feel. Generous use of white space, clean typography, product photos and conversion-focused elements like badges, reviews and delivery icons.
caseMaxled.mobile_title: Optimized for mobile use on location.
caseMaxled.mobile_p1: A large part of the target audience visits the website on the go or at event locations. The mobile experience is therefore fully optimized for fast navigation and direct action.
caseMaxled.mobile_p2: Touch-friendly elements, a simplified menu and prominent CTAs ensure visitors can quickly request a quote on mobile too.
caseMaxled.result_title: A webshop that builds trust and converts.
caseMaxled.result_p1: The end result is a fully responsive e-commerce platform that performs strongly on every device. Product pages, rental formulas and the assortment are clearly and convincingly presented.
caseMaxled.result_p2: The new webshop strengthens MaxLED's professional image and provides a solid foundation for further growth in both rental and sales.

caseBouwmeester.label: Construction & Installation · Web Design
caseBouwmeester.title: Bouwmeester
caseBouwmeester.description: Professional website for a plumbing and installation company in the Zwolle region. Focus on services, trust and local findability.
caseBouwmeester.opdracht_title: A reliable online appearance for a craftsman.
caseBouwmeester.opdracht_p1: Bouwmeester Installatietechniek is a plumbing and installation company in the Zwolle region. They needed a professional website that reflects their craftsmanship and reliability.
caseBouwmeester.opdracht_p2: The objective was clear: generate more inquiries through the website, improve local findability and build trust with potential customers.
caseBouwmeester.aanpak_title: Structure, trust and conversion as guidelines.
caseBouwmeester.aanpak_p1: We built the website around the company's core values: craftsmanship, reliability and fast service. The sitemap focuses on clear services and accessible contact.
caseBouwmeester.aanpak_p2: With strong photography, clear texts and prominent call-to-actions, the visitor is quickly guided to a contact moment.
caseBouwmeester.resultaat_title: A website that builds trust and converts.
caseBouwmeester.resultaat_p1: The end result is a fully responsive website that looks professional on every device. The projects page and expertise give visitors immediate insight into Bouwmeester's work.
caseBouwmeester.resultaat_p2: The website is optimized for local findability and has contributed to a significant increase in online inquiries.

caseLohues.label: Energy & Sustainability · UI/UX Design · Web Design
caseLohues.title: Lohues Installation Technology
caseLohues.description: Complete website for a family business in sustainability. From solar panels to heat pumps — everything under one roof with focus on trust, local findability and conversion.
caseLohues.story_title: A family business making sustainability accessible across the Netherlands.
caseLohues.story_p1: Lohues Installation Technology is a family business with over 25 installers working nationwide. They install solar panels, air conditioning, batteries, heat pumps, fuse boxes and charging stations.
caseLohues.story_p2: The assignment: a complete website that clearly presents their broad range of services, builds trust with both private and business customers, and guides visitors to a quote request or free inspection.
caseLohues.aanpak_title: Structure, trust and conversion as foundation.
caseLohues.aanpak_p1: The website is built around the company's core values: craftsmanship, honesty and fast service. Each page has a clear hierarchy with prominent call-to-actions for free inspections and quotes.
caseLohues.aanpak_p2: The service pages combine FAQ sections with benefits and a direct contact form, so visitors find all information without leaving the page.
caseLohues.trust_title: Personal story and certifications build trust.
caseLohues.trust_p1: The about page opens with a personal message from director Leroy, complete with portrait photo and signature. This gives visitors an immediate warm welcome and human connection.
caseLohues.trust_p2: Certifications from partners like Enphase, GoodWe, DMEGC and JinKO are prominently displayed. The team overview with photos and roles makes the company tangible and trustworthy.
caseLohues.projects_p1: The projects page shows completed work filtered by category — from solar panels to charging stations. Visitors immediately see the breadth and quality of the work.
caseLohues.projects_p2: Statistics like "2,245 homes and businesses" and "28,560 installed solar panels" serve as powerful social proof and strengthen credibility.
caseLohues.conversion_p1: The contact form promises "response within 24 hours" and is accompanied by trust signals: always non-binding, no call-out charges, completely free and everywhere in the Netherlands.
caseLohues.conversion_p2: The quote page is designed as a step-by-step form — enter zip code and house number, and within a minute the request is complete. Minimal barrier, maximum conversion.

caseTheoriedoen.label: EdTech · UI/UX Design · Web Design · Branding
caseTheoriedoen.title: Theoriedoen.be
caseTheoriedoen.description: Complete redesign of the #1 driving theory platform in Belgium. From landing page to social media — a cohesive brand that appeals to young people and converts.
caseTheoriedoen.story_title: The #1 platform for driving theory in Belgium, ready for the next step.
caseTheoriedoen.story_p1: Theoriedoen.be helps thousands of young people in Belgium pass their driving theory exam. With over 50,000 successful students and a 99% pass rate, they are the market leader in their segment.
caseTheoriedoen.story_p2: The assignment: a complete visual redesign that makes the platform more modern, trustworthy and conversion-focused — including landing page, social media content and a recognizable brand identity.
caseTheoriedoen.design_p1: The design appeals to the young target audience with a powerful blue-yellow color palette, dynamic photography and a playful yet professional tone of voice. Every section is optimized for conversion.
caseTheoriedoen.design_p2: From pricing tables with clear comparison to social proof with reviews and statistics — every element guides visitors toward sign-up.
caseTheoriedoen.landing_title: A homepage that convinces with every scroll.
caseTheoriedoen.landing_p1: The landing page opens with a powerful hero: "Ready to pass your driving theory?" Immediately followed by social proof — 50,993 students, 99% pass rate and a 4.8/5 rating.
caseTheoriedoen.landing_p2: Below follow sections for exam categories, the step-by-step plan, reviews from real users, a free demo offer and transparent pricing. Everything is aimed at removing doubt.
caseTheoriedoen.social_p1: In addition to the website, a complete set of social media templates was designed. From Instagram stories to Facebook ads — every expression carries the same recognizable blue-yellow DNA.
caseTheoriedoen.social_p2: The templates are designed for variation within consistency: different formats and messages, but always unmistakably Theoriedoen.be.

caseBatterij.label: Sustainability · Web Design & Branding
caseBatterij.title: Batterij-recyclen.nl
caseBatterij.description: Platform for responsible battery management and recycling. A mission-driven website contributing to the transition to a green economy.
caseBatterij.opdracht_p1: Batterij-recyclen.nl had a clear mission: increasing awareness around responsible battery management and guiding businesses in the transition to a circular economy.
caseBatterij.opdracht_p2: The challenge was to transform complex information about recycling processes and regulations into a clear, visually attractive platform that appeals to both businesses and consumers.
caseBatterij.inspiratie_title: Nature as visual language for technology.
caseBatterij.inspiratie_p1: The visual direction is inspired by the interplay between technology and nature. We chose a dark color palette combined with vibrant green — a direct reference to sustainability and green energy.
caseBatterij.inspiratie_p2: Organic shapes break through the clean structure and refer to the circular movement of recycling. Images of wind turbines and green landscapes reinforce the story of a cleaner future.
caseBatterij.inspiratie_p3: The result is a brand identity that radiates trust and innovation — fitting for an organization at the forefront of the transition to responsible battery management.
caseBatterij.aanpak_title: Information architecture with a green mission.
caseBatterij.aanpak_p1: The website is built around three pillars: inform, activate and connect. Visitors are guided step by step through the recycling process, from collection points to the impact of responsible battery management.
caseBatterij.aanpak_p2: With prominent call-to-actions, an interactive collection points map and FAQ section, the barrier to taking action is kept as low as possible.
caseBatterij.resultaat_title: A platform that makes sustainability tangible.
caseBatterij.resultaat_p1: The end result is a fully responsive platform that presents complex sustainability information in an accessible way. The website combines education with action.
caseBatterij.resultaat_p2: The powerful visual identity — dark tones with fresh green — ensures recognition and distinguishes Batterij-recyclen.nl as an authority in the sector.

caseSoortenregister.label: Government & Science · UX/UI Design
caseSoortenregister.title: Dutch Species Register
caseSoortenregister.description: Central database for all known Dutch species — with standardized reference data, photos, sounds and DNA barcodes.
caseSoortenregister.opdracht_title: Making biodiversity data accessible for everyone.
caseSoortenregister.opdracht_p1: The Dutch Species Register is the central database for Dutch biodiversity, managed by Naturalis Biodiversity Center. With over 14 million species and millions of barcodes, sounds and photos, it is an indispensable resource for researchers, policymakers and nature enthusiasts.
caseSoortenregister.opdracht_p2: The challenge: transforming an enormous amount of scientific data into an intuitive, modern interface that serves both experts and the general public.
caseSoortenregister.ux_p1: We completely restructured the information architecture. Users can discover species through visual categories, taxonomic trees or targeted searches — each path leads to the same rich detail pages.
caseSoortenregister.ux_p2: Filters, display modes (images, list, table) and smart search suggestions make navigating through millions of records intuitive and fast.
caseSoortenregister.detail_title: Every species deserves a complete story.
caseSoortenregister.detail_p1: The species page bundles all available data into one clear overview: photos, sounds, distribution, biotopes, food, DNA sequences and related species. Tabs allow quick switching between summary, literature, specimens and location data.
caseSoortenregister.detail_p2: The design balances scientific depth with visual appeal — so both a biologist and a curious visitor can find their way.
caseSoortenregister.resultaat_title: A modern gateway to Dutch biodiversity.
caseSoortenregister.resultaat_p1: The renewed platform makes it possible for the first time to browse through millions of species in a visually attractive way. The collaboration with Naturalis, EIS Knowledge Centre, Waarneming.nl and the Dutch Government guarantees reliable, up-to-date data.
caseSoortenregister.resultaat_p2: The result is a platform that combines scientific precision with user-friendly design — a bridge between science and society.

caseArise.label: UX Case Study · UI/UX Design · Prototype
caseArise.description: UX case study and prototype for the ARISE platform — an ambitious biodiversity project that maps all multicellular species in the Netherlands with AI-powered recognition.
caseArise.about_p1: An organized overview of all multicellular life forms in the Netherlands and the infrastructure to semi-automatically identify all these species. That is what the ARISE project aims to achieve.
caseArise.about_p2: Naturalis Biodiversity Center, the University of Amsterdam, the Westerdijk Fungal Biodiversity Institute and the University of Twente are collaborating on a biodiversity monitoring infrastructure that will be the first of its kind.
caseArise.about_p3: ARISE will become an indispensable tool for people in nature management, ecological research and natural history museums from around the world.
caseArise.diopsis_p1: Diopsis is a camera specifically designed to photograph insects and then perform species recognition based on these photos.
caseArise.diopsis_p2: An existing workflow already exists for this system — from taking the photo with the camera, to a system where experts can annotate the photos and an AI model that automatically recognizes species, to a report for the end user.
caseArise.diopsis_p3: However, there is currently no place where this data resides or where users can interact with it. This user portal will become part of the larger ARISE platform.
caseArise.lean_description: In the Lean Canvas we discussed the main objectives and user goals for this project. What are the goals and when do we achieve them?
caseArise.lean_quote: "We as ARISE want to become the Spotify and Google Analytics for biodiversity in The Netherlands."
caseArise.empathy_description: To better understand the needs of our users, we created an empathy map based on interviews with ecologists and researchers.
caseArise.hypotheses_description: Based on our user research, we formulated six core hypotheses around filter systems, data access, customization, comparison, visualization and reporting.
caseArise.cardsorting_description: Through card sorting, we mapped the most important functionalities: search, view data, compare, download and upload.
caseArise.userflow_description: We designed detailed user flows for both user groups — the Data Explorer and the Ecologist with Diopsis camera.
caseArise.wireframes_description: We started with low-fidelity wireframes to validate the information structure and layout before moving to high-fidelity designs.
caseArise.hifi_description: The wireframes were translated into a fully high-fidelity prototype with search functionality, data browsing, species detail pages and a dashboard.
caseArise.testing_description: We tested four core areas with real users: the landing page, the filter feature, the compare feature and data visualization.
caseArise.conclusion_p1: The end result is a high-fidelity prototype that translates the complex world of biodiversity data into an intuitive, modern interface. The prototype has been validated with real users and serves as a blueprint for the further development of the ARISE platform.
caseArise.conclusion_p2: User tests confirmed that the platform finds the right balance between scientific depth and ease of use — a crucial factor for broad adoption by the research community.

caseJawel.label: Branding & Web Design
caseJawel.description: Development of a powerful brand identity and modern website for an infrastructure company from Zeeland. Focus on reliability, strength and Zeeland roots.
caseJawel.intro_p1: Jawel Infra needed a professional brand identity that matches their no-nonsense, reliable approach in the infrastructure sector. The goal: a recognizable brand that radiates trust and fits the mentality of infrastructure work.
caseJawel.intro_p2: The project encompassed the full development of logo, house style, iconography, photography and a modern website — aimed at attracting both private and commercial assignments.

values.title: Powerful
values.desc: Broad, geometric shapes and bold typography for a strong impression

values.title: Reliable
values.desc: Clean structure, clear hierarchy and rhythm

values.title: Zeeland Roots
values.desc: Wave shapes as subtle link to water, calm and stability

caseJawel.typo_description: Barlow Condensed as a powerful display typeface, combined with Barlow and Inter for body text. The color palette — black (#0F0F10), orange (#EC5406) and white — reflects power and energy.
caseJawel.photo_description: Professional on-location photography that captures the power and craftsmanship of infrastructure work. The branded workwear with the new logo strengthens recognition in the field.
caseJawel.web_description: A modern, dark website that reflects the brand's power. With dynamic orange accents, bold typography and professional photography as core visuals.
caseJawel.social_description: Social media templates and campaign materials that consistently translate the brand identity to digital channels and recruitment campaigns.
